architecture.summary: MPnP connects data encoders (for diverse input modalities) to flexible positions across the last blocks of a frozen LLM, rather than only to the input layer. The connection points and their weights are made fully trainable at runtime, allowing the model to adaptively select which modalities to route into which LLM layers based on environmental context and task requirements. Information flows from per-modality encoders into learned adapter projections that inject activations into mid-to-late transformer blocks of the host LLM.
trained_components: routing/gating weights for runtime modality selection

biblio.title: Modality Plug-and-Play: Runtime Modality Adaptation in LLM-Driven Autonomous Mobile Systems

claims.statement: 3.7× FLOPs reduction compared to best baselines
30% memory usage reduction compared to best baselines
requires only few hundreds of training samples at runtime
completes modality adaptation within few minutes on weak devices
high compute and data efficiency compared to fixed-connection baselines that train encoders only at the LLM input layer
